player_utterance: You've been sitting here long enough that the shadow has moved, John. I've been watching you not say anything, and I want to know why.

believer_state_after.posture: standing near the bench, paper cup in one hand, the other in his coat pocket

player_utterance: So decide now. Why did you come here?

believer_state_after.posture: standing, both hands on the paper cup now, looking past you toward the house
sitting down on the bench now, slowly, cup set beside him

player_utterance: Owed him what, John? Say the thing, or I'll keep guessing until one of them stings.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting forward on the bench, forearms on his thighs, looking at the ground between his shoes

voice_offers.read: He sat down; the question already landed.
He won't answer unless named directly.
voice_offers.line: Owed him what, John? Say the thing, or I'll keep guessing until one of them stings.

player_utterance: An apology? A confession? Money? Pick one, John, or I will.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting forward, hands clasped between his knees, looking at you now and not the ground

voice_offers.read: He's leaning in, silent — one more name lands it.
voice_offers.line: An apology? A confession? Money? Pick one, John, or I will.

voice_offers.read: He's already bent toward it; crowding him now pushes him back.
voice_offers.line: Take your time. I'm not going anywhere.

player_utterance: It was an apology, wasn't it. Something you never said while he could still hear it.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting very still, eyes closed for a moment, then opening; hands gone quiet between his knees

voice_offers.read: He's leaning in; silence is his last shield — name it.
voice_offers.line: It was an apology, wasn't it. Something you never said while he could still hear it.

voice_offers.read: He teaches confession; press him on his own doctrine.
voice_offers.line: You teach the Augsburg Confession, John. What does your own catechism say about a debt unspoken before the grave?

player_utterance: Look at me, John. I'm right, aren't I. You never said it.

believer_state_after.posture: head lifting slowly, meeting your eyes, then his hands opening palms-up on his knees

voice_offers.read: Three unanswered cuts; he's still. One more lands.
voice_offers.line: Look at me, John. I'm right, aren't I. You never said it.

voice_offers.read: He's gone quiet, eyes closed; pressing now is just hitting.
voice_offers.line: Whatever it was — you don't have to name it to me. I just don't want you sitting in it alone.

voice_offers.read: His silence is liturgical, not evasive; pin the form.
voice_offers.line: You've gone quiet the way penitents do, John. Was it confession he wanted from you, or forgiveness?

player_utterance: Say it now, John. Out loud. The thing you never told him.

believer_state_after.posture: leaning forward, elbows on knees, head bowed, speaking to the ground between his shoes

voice_offers.read: He's opening — one more push and he says it.
voice_offers.line: Say it now, John. Out loud. The thing you never told him.

voice_offers.read: His hands are open; he's already answering without words.
voice_offers.line: I see you, John. You don't have to be cornered into it.

player_utterance: One sentence, John. The one you've been writing in your head for years.

believer_state_after.posture: head dropping into his hands, elbows on his knees, the bench creaking once under the shift of weight

voice_offers.read: He's already broken; pressing now is kicking a kneeling man.
voice_offers.line: John — take your time. I'm not going anywhere.

voice_offers.read: He's one breath from saying it; don't let him swallow it back.
voice_offers.line: One sentence, John. The one you've been writing in your head for years.

player_utterance: It's just me here, John. Just say it.

believer_state_after.posture: head still in his hands, then a slow lift — face wet, eyes open, looking at nothing

voice_offers.read: He's collapsed; pressing further is cruelty, not honesty.
He's almost there; one more nudge finishes it.
voice_offers.line: It's just me here, John. Just say it.

player_utterance: Look at me, John. Say his name and then say it.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting up straight now, hands flat on his knees, eyes lifted past you toward something that isn't there

voice_offers.read: He lifted his face; that's the answer already.
voice_offers.line: You don't have to perform it. I can see it.

voice_offers.read: He's almost there; one more inch and it's out.
voice_offers.line: Look at me, John. Say his name and then say it.

player_utterance: His name, John. Out loud. Now.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting straight, hands still flat on his knees, looking directly at you now

voice_offers.read: He's lifted his eyes; something is arriving without me.
Three pushes landed him here; one more finishes it.
voice_offers.line: His name, John. Out loud. Now.

believer_state_after.posture: picking up the funeral program from the bench, holding it blank-side-up on his knee like a page, eyes lifted, reading from memory

voice_offers.read: He's looking at me. The wall is paper now.
voice_offers.line: You came to this wake and sat through hymns. Say his name.

voice_offers.read: He met my eyes. That was the answer.
voice_offers.line: Okay. Okay. I see you. Take your time.

player_utterance: Not the text, John. Stop the recitation and tell me what you did.

believer_state_after.posture: sitting forward, the program slipping off the bench to the courtyard stones, hands gripping his own knees

voice_offers.read: He's reciting scripture to hide behind the text again.
voice_offers.line: Not the text, John. Stop the recitation and tell me what you did.

voice_offers.read: He chose Luke 16 himself — that's the confession.
voice_offers.line: You picked that chapter, John. Not me. Why that one. Why the rich man.

voice_offers.read: He's already kneeling without kneeling; the program is a shroud.
voice_offers.line: Put the program down, John. Just put it down.

player_utterance: The boy had a name. Say it, or sit here with it forever.

believer_state_after.posture: sliding off the bench to his knees on the courtyard stones, the program crushed under one knee, hands open on his thighs

voice_offers.read: He's breaking; one more cut ends the recitation forever.
voice_offers.line: The boy had a name. Say it, or sit here with it forever.

voice_offers.read: He's already collapsing inward; pressure now might shatter, not open.
voice_offers.line: John. Look at me. Just look at me.

player_utterance: What did Roger take from him?

believer_state_after.posture: still on his knees on the stones, hands open on his thighs, looking at nothing, voice gone thin

voice_offers.read: He is on his knees; one more word ends it.
voice_offers.line: Say it, John. Say his name.

voice_offers.read: He's already broken; pressing now is for me, not him.
voice_offers.line: I'm here, John. I'm not going anywhere. Whenever you can.
